title: What Tools Does a Professional Kitchen Fitter Use?
metaDescription: Professional kitchen fitters use laser levels, circular saws, routers, drills and specialist jigs. Quality tools are essential for a professional finish. From Install My Kitchen's 30 years of experience.
answer: A professional kitchen fitter uses laser levels, spirit levels, circular saw, jigsaw, drill, router, mitre saw, clamps, packing pieces, silicone gun, and a comprehensive set of hand tools. Quality tools are essential for accurate cuts, precise levelling, and a professional finish.
bodyHtml:
Levelling and Measuring Tools

Every kitchen installation begins with achieving a perfectly level and plumb starting point, and the tools used for this task have a direct impact on the finished result. A professional fitter carries a self-levelling laser level that projects both horizontal and vertical lines simultaneously across the room. This allows every unit to be referenced against a single datum, rather than relying on the floor or walls, which are rarely straight. A good-quality spirit level is used alongside the laser for individual cabinet checks. Measuring tapes, combination squares, and marking gauges complete the measuring toolkit, ensuring every cut and position is precise before work begins.

Cutting Tools and Saws

Accurate cutting is one of the most demanding aspects of kitchen fitting. A circular saw with a fine-tooth blade makes long, straight cuts in carcasses and worktops. A jigsaw is used for curved cuts and sink cut-outs. A router is essential for scribing and shaping filler panels, worktop jointing, and creating precise edging. A mitre saw handles the clean 45-degree cuts needed for cornice and pelmet joints. Every saw must be sharp — a blunt blade tears rather than cuts, and torn edges show in the finished kitchen.

What Separates a Professional's Toolkit

Beyond the core tools, experienced fitters carry specialist jigs for worktop jointing, hinge-drilling templates, drawer runner alignment tools, and a comprehensive silicone and adhesive kit. Over 30 years, Pindi Sahota at Install My Kitchen has invested in a professional toolkit that covers every eventuality — from scribing a curved wall to fitting an integrated appliance housing. The difference between a professional and a DIY installation often comes down not to knowledge alone, but to having the right tool for every task and knowing precisely how to use it.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter need a laser level?
faqs.a: A laser level is one of the most important tools a professional fitter uses. It projects a level reference line across the entire room, ensuring every unit is installed to the same datum regardless of floor or wall imperfections.

faqs.q: What tool is used to cut worktops?
faqs.a: A circular saw with a fine-tooth blade is used for straight worktop cuts. A jigsaw is used for sink cut-outs and awkward shapes. A router is used to create worktop joint recesses and for scribing edges to walls.

faqs.q: Do professional kitchen fitters bring their own tools?
faqs.a: Yes — a professional fitter brings all necessary tools. You should never need to provide anything beyond access, power sockets, and adequate lighting. If a fitter asks to borrow your tools, that is a warning sign.

faqs.q: What is a hinge-drilling jig used for?
faqs.a: A hinge-drilling jig ensures every hinge cup hole is drilled at exactly the same position on every door, guaranteeing consistent door alignment across the entire kitchen. Without a jig, each hole position must be individually measured, increasing the risk of inconsistency.

title: How Are Stone Worktops Measured and Fitted?
metaDescription: Stone worktops are measured by templating — a specialist fabricator visits after base units are installed and returns 5-10 days later to fit. Co-ordinated by Install My Kitchen.
answer: Stone worktops are measured by templating — a specialist fabricator visits after base units are installed, creates a precise template, and takes it to their workshop where the stone is cut. The fitted worktop is returned and installed 5–10 working days later.
bodyHtml:
Why Templating Is Necessary

Stone worktops — whether granite, quartz, or marble — cannot be measured from drawings alone. Every kitchen has slight variations in wall angles, unit positions, and dimensions that only become apparent once the base units are installed. Templating is the process of creating a precise, physical template of the worktop area so that the stone slab can be cut exactly to fit. The template accounts for every corner angle, appliance cut-out, sink position, and wall scribe. Without accurate templating, even an expensive stone worktop will have visible gaps or joints that do not close cleanly.

The Templating Visit — What Happens

Once all base units are installed and perfectly levelled, the stone fabricator visits the property. Using either rigid cardboard templates or a digital laser templating system, they measure every surface precisely, including upstands, windowsill returns, and any curved walls. Sink and hob cut-outs are marked. The template is then taken back to the fabricator's workshop where computer-controlled machinery cuts the stone slab to the exact specification. Edge profiles are applied, and any polishing is completed before the worktop returns for installation.

Lead Times and Programme Planning

The standard lead time between templating and installation is 5–10 working days, though premium or less common stone types may take longer. This gap must be planned into the overall kitchen programme. At Install My Kitchen, we advise clients to co-ordinate the templating visit at the end of the base unit installation, so the plumber can connect the sink immediately once the stone arrives. Planning this sequence carefully avoids leaving the kitchen without a working sink for longer than necessary.

faqs.q: Can stone worktops be measured before units are installed?
faqs.a: No — stone worktops must be templated after base units are installed. Even small discrepancies between drawings and actual unit positions would result in a poorly fitting stone top. Templating is always done on the installed units.

faqs.q: How long does templating take?
faqs.a: A templating visit typically takes 1–2 hours depending on the complexity and size of the kitchen. Kitchens with curved walls, island units, or complex corner arrangements take longer than straightforward linear layouts.

faqs.q: Who arranges the stone templating?
faqs.a: Templating is arranged by the stone fabricator, not the kitchen fitter. You should have your fabricator booked and ready before installation begins so they can visit as soon as the base units are complete.

faqs.q: Can you use the kitchen while waiting for stone worktops?
faqs.a: In most cases, a temporary worktop is not fitted during the wait. The kitchen is not fully functional during this period. We advise planning for up to two weeks without a complete kitchen when stone worktops are part of your project.

title: What Is a Soft-Close Hinge on a Kitchen?
metaDescription: Soft-close hinges have built-in dampers that prevent cabinet doors from slamming. They require calibration during installation. Most modern kitchens include them as standard.
answer: A soft-close hinge has a built-in damper that slows and cushions the door as it closes, preventing slamming. Most modern kitchen ranges include soft-close hinges as standard. They require calibration during installation — tension and positioning are adjusted for each door.
bodyHtml:
How Soft-Close Hinges Work

A soft-close hinge contains a small hydraulic or spring-loaded damper inside the hinge mechanism. As the door reaches the last 15–20 degrees of its closing arc, the damper engages and slows the door to a gentle, controlled close. This prevents the slamming that was characteristic of older kitchens and reduces wear on both the hinge and the cabinet frame. The mechanism is entirely self-contained within the hinge body — no additional components are required. Most quality kitchen ranges from Howdens, Wren, Magnet, and other leading suppliers include soft-close hinges as standard across their product range.

Calibrating Hinges During Installation

Soft-close hinges have multiple adjustment points — typically three: height, depth, and lateral position. These adjustments allow the door to be aligned perfectly in all three dimensions. During installation, each door must be individually calibrated so that it sits level with adjacent doors, closes flush with the cabinet frame, and has even gaps on all sides. A professional fitter checks every door from multiple angles and makes micro-adjustments until the alignment is consistent across the entire kitchen. This calibration process is time-consuming but essential — a kitchen with poorly aligned doors looks unfinished regardless of how good the units themselves are.

What to Do If Soft-Close Fails

Soft-close dampers can fail over time, particularly if doors are repeatedly slammed open against the damper's resistance. When a hinge loses its soft-close function, the hinge itself can usually be replaced without disturbing the door or cabinet. In most cases, the replacement hinge clips directly onto the existing mounting plate. If you notice a door slamming or feel the damper has weakened on your existing kitchen, contact Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836 — hinge replacement is a straightforward job that takes only a few minutes per door.

faqs.q: Do all modern kitchens come with soft-close hinges?
faqs.a: Most modern mid-range and premium kitchen ranges include soft-close hinges as standard. Budget ranges may use standard hinges. If you are unsure, check the hinge specification with your retailer before purchasing.

faqs.q: Can soft-close hinges be added to an existing kitchen?
faqs.a: Yes — soft-close hinges can be retrofitted to an existing kitchen by replacing the existing hinges. The new hinges must be compatible with the existing mounting plates. This is a relatively simple job for an experienced fitter.

faqs.q: Why do some kitchen doors not close properly after installation?
faqs.a: Doors that do not close properly after installation are typically out of adjustment. The three adjustment screws on each hinge control height, depth, and side-to-side position. A professional fitter calibrates all doors before handing over the kitchen.

faqs.q: How many hinges does a standard kitchen door need?
faqs.a: Most standard-height kitchen doors use two hinges. Taller doors over 1200mm typically use three hinges to prevent warping and ensure smooth, consistent closing. The third hinge is fitted centrally between the upper and lower hinges.

title: In-Frame vs Shaker Kitchen — Which Is Harder to Install?
metaDescription: In-frame kitchens take 20-30% longer to install than shaker kitchens and require higher precision. Shaker is more forgiving. Advice from Install My Kitchen's 30-year Coventry fitter.
answer: In-frame kitchens are significantly more complex to install than shaker kitchens. The solid timber frame requires precise scribing, exact door-to-frame gap consistency, and careful levelling. In-frame installations typically take 20–30% longer and demand higher skill levels.
bodyHtml:
What Makes In-Frame Construction Different

A shaker kitchen has a simple construction — the door sits on the face of the cabinet carcass, attached by adjustable hinges. An in-frame kitchen is fundamentally different. Each cabinet has a solid timber or MDF frame fixed to the front of the carcass, and the door sits within this frame rather than over it. The result is a furniture-like appearance with visible frame reveals around each door — but achieving that appearance requires a level of precision that shaker installations simply do not demand. The frame must be square, level, and perfectly aligned, and the doors must be hung with entirely consistent gaps on all four sides.

Why In-Frame Takes Longer

The complexity of in-frame installation lies in several additional steps. The frame components must be cut, mitred, and assembled before doors can be hung. Any variation in the frame — even a millimetre — is clearly visible in the door-to-frame gap. Scribing an in-frame kitchen to an uneven wall requires removing material from the frame itself, which must be done carefully to avoid weakening it. Adjustable hinges are not always used in in-frame construction; some manufacturers use traditional butt hinges that have no adjustment, making precise positioning even more critical. These factors together add 20–30% to the installation time compared to an equivalent shaker kitchen.

Choosing the Right Fitter for In-Frame

In-frame kitchens should only be installed by experienced kitchen fitters with a proven record of in-frame work. Ask to see completed examples before committing. At Install My Kitchen, Pindi Sahota has installed in-frame kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire throughout his 30-year career and understands the additional care and time that genuine in-frame construction demands. If you are investing in an in-frame kitchen, it is worth taking extra care in choosing the right installer — the quality of the installation determines whether the kitchen lives up to its premium appearance.

faqs.q: Is an in-frame kitchen worth the extra cost?
faqs.a: An in-frame kitchen provides a premium, furniture-like appearance that shaker construction cannot match. If the look is important to you and your budget allows, the additional cost of both the units and the installation is worthwhile for the finished result.

faqs.q: Can any kitchen fitter install an in-frame kitchen?
faqs.a: In theory yes, but in practice you should choose a fitter with specific in-frame experience. The tolerances are much tighter than shaker installation, and mistakes are difficult to correct without damaging the frame.

faqs.q: Are shaker kitchens easier to install than in-frame?
faqs.a: Yes — shaker kitchens are more forgiving than in-frame. Adjustable hinges allow door alignment to be corrected after fitting, and small gaps can be concealed by the door overlay. In-frame construction leaves nowhere to hide imprecision.

faqs.q: How do I tell the difference between in-frame and shaker?
faqs.a: On an in-frame kitchen, you will see a solid frame surrounding each door, with a consistent reveal visible when the door is closed. On a shaker kitchen, the door sits flush against or slightly overlapping the cabinet front with no visible frame around the door.

title: Which Kitchen Retailers Allow Independent Fitters?
metaDescription: All major UK retailers — Wren, Howdens, Magnet, IKEA, Wickes, B&Q, Benchmarx and all others — allow independent installation. Unit warranties are not affected by who installs.
answer: All major UK kitchen retailers — Wren, Howdens, Magnet, IKEA, Wickes, B&Q, Benchmarx, DIY Kitchens and all others — allow installation by independent fitters. No retailer requires you to use their own service. Unit warranties are not affected by who installs.
bodyHtml:
Retailer-by-Retailer Confirmation

There is a common misconception that using a retailer's own installation service is required to maintain the unit warranty. This is not the case for any major UK kitchen retailer. Wren Kitchens sells installation as an optional add-on service but explicitly does not require it. Howdens Joinery has no consumer installation service at all — the trade-only model means independent fitters are the only route for homeowners. Magnet, Wickes, B&Q, IKEA, Benchmarx, DIY Kitchens, and every other significant retailer all permit independent installation without any impact on unit warranties.

What Unit Warranties Actually Cover

Kitchen unit warranties from retailers cover manufacturing defects — issues such as a door that warps without cause, a drawer box that fails, or a hinge that breaks under normal use. These defects are the manufacturer's responsibility regardless of who installed the kitchen. Warranties do not cover damage caused during installation, such as a broken hinge cup or a cracked carcass side — those would be the installer's responsibility. Understanding this distinction is important: a retailer warranty and an installation workmanship warranty are separate things covering different aspects of the kitchen.

How to Use an Independent Fitter

Using an independent fitter is straightforward. Purchase your kitchen from your chosen retailer, arrange your own delivery, and engage an independent fitter to carry out the installation. The fitter will need the full delivery packing list to check for missing or damaged items before work begins. Install My Kitchen provides independent installation of all major kitchen brands across Coventry and Warwickshire. Call 07399 651836 to arrange a survey and fixed quote.

faqs.q: Does using an independent fitter void my kitchen warranty?
faqs.a: No — unit warranties from all major retailers cover manufacturing defects regardless of who installs the kitchen. The warranty is tied to the product, not to the installer. Damage caused during installation would be the installer's responsibility, not the retailer's.

faqs.q: Does Howdens have their own installation service?
faqs.a: No — Howdens is a trade-only supplier and has no consumer installation service. Homeowners purchasing through a Howdens account must use an independent fitter or builder to carry out the installation.

faqs.q: Can I use an independent fitter for an IKEA kitchen?
faqs.a: Yes — IKEA does not require you to use their own installation service. Any independent fitter can install an IKEA kitchen. IKEA's own service is simply one option among many.

faqs.q: Why use an independent fitter over a retailer's own service?
faqs.a: Independent fitters often offer more competitive pricing, greater flexibility, and a more personal service. You can also read independent reviews and check previous work before committing, rather than relying on a large retailer's subcontracted workforce.

title: What Is a Supply and Fit Kitchen?
metaDescription: Supply and fit means one company supplies and installs the kitchen. Supply only means you buy from a retailer and arrange independent installation. Both have advantages.
answer: Supply and fit means one company supplies the units and installs them. Supply only means you buy the kitchen yourself and arrange installation separately. Supply and fit is convenient but limits your fitter choice. Supply only gives full control over both brand and installer.
bodyHtml:
Supply and Fit — Pros and Cons

A supply and fit kitchen is a one-stop arrangement where the same company designs, supplies, and installs the kitchen. Retailers such as John Lewis, Magnet, and certain bespoke companies offer this route. The convenience is the main advantage — you deal with one company throughout, and responsibility for both the products and the installation sits in one place. The disadvantage is that you are limited to their product range, their pricing, and their installation workforce. You have less ability to compare installation quality independently, and the combined cost is often higher than sourcing the two elements separately.

Supply Only — Pros and Cons

Supply only means you purchase the kitchen from a retailer — Wren, Howdens, IKEA, Benchmarx, or any other — and separately engage an independent fitter. This gives you complete control. You can choose the best kitchen for your budget from any range available, and you can independently assess and select your installer based on reviews, previous work, and fixed quotes. Responsibility is clearly divided: the retailer covers unit defects, the fitter covers workmanship. The main disadvantage is that you manage two separate relationships, and co-ordination between delivery and installation requires more planning.

Which Route Is Right for You

For most homeowners in Coventry and Warwickshire, the supply-only route offers better value and greater control. Choosing your kitchen independently from a retailer such as Howdens or Wren, then engaging Install My Kitchen for installation, allows you to be confident in both the product and the installer. Pindi Sahota has installed kitchens from every major UK supplier over 30 years — call 07399 651836 to discuss the best approach for your project.

faqs.q: Is supply and fit always more expensive?
faqs.a: Not always, but supply and fit packages from major retailers are often priced at a premium for the convenience. Getting separate quotes for units and installation allows you to compare the total cost directly.

faqs.q: Who is responsible if something goes wrong with a supply and fit kitchen?
faqs.a: With supply and fit, one company is responsible for both product and installation. This simplifies disputes but means you have less ability to separate a product defect from an installation error independently.

faqs.q: Can I buy from Howdens and use my own fitter?
faqs.a: Yes — this is the standard route for Howdens kitchens. Howdens has no consumer installation service, so independent fitters are used by the vast majority of homeowners who purchase through Howdens.

faqs.q: What does Install My Kitchen supply?
faqs.a: Install My Kitchen is an installation service — we fit kitchens you have purchased from any retailer. We do not supply units. This means you choose the kitchen you want, and we install it to a professional standard.

title: Do Kitchen Fitters Do Tiling?
metaDescription: Some kitchen fitters offer tiling as an optional service. Install My Kitchen offers wall and floor tiling as an optional extra across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Some kitchen fitters offer tiling as an optional service. Install My Kitchen offers wall and floor tiling as an optional extra. Wall tiling around splashbacks typically happens after units are in; floor tiling is usually done before units are installed.
bodyHtml:
Wall Tiling — When It Happens

Wall tiling in a kitchen is typically carried out after the base and wall units are installed but before the worktops are fitted. Tiling the splashback area at this stage means the tiles can be cut neatly to the underside of the wall units and the top of the base units, with the worktop covering the bottom edge of the tiles. Attempting to tile before units are in place requires more precise measurement and often results in cuts that do not align cleanly with the units. There are exceptions — some clients prefer to tile the full wall before installation — and the approach will be agreed during the survey.

Floor Tiling Timing

Floor tiling, where it is part of the project, is usually completed before kitchen units are installed. This avoids the need to cut tiles around the base of every unit and underneath the plinth area. An uninterrupted tiled floor also makes future changes easier — if a unit is ever moved or replaced, the floor beneath is already tiled. In practice, this means floor tiling must be completed, grouted, and fully cured before the kitchen fitter returns to begin cabinet installation. The survey will confirm the correct sequence for your specific project.

Tiling as an Optional Addition

Not all kitchen fitters offer tiling. Install My Kitchen provides wall and floor tiling as an optional service alongside kitchen installation, removing the need to coordinate a separate tiler. This simplifies the project timeline and ensures the tiling and installation work together as part of a single, co-ordinated programme. To discuss tiling as part of your kitchen installation in Coventry or Warwickshire, call 07399 651836.

faqs.q: Should tiles go under kitchen units or stop at the plinth?
faqs.a: Tiles should ideally run under the units, or at least to the line where the plinth will sit. This ensures that if units are ever moved or replaced, the floor is not left with bare patches. Full floor tiling before unit installation is the cleanest approach.

faqs.q: Can I tile my own splashback after the kitchen is fitted?
faqs.a: Yes — many homeowners choose to tile their own splashback after the kitchen is complete. The tiles are fitted to the wall between the worktop and the underside of the wall units. This is a reasonable DIY task if you are comfortable with basic tiling.

faqs.q: Do I need to remove old tiles before a new kitchen is installed?
faqs.a: Old tiles behind units do not always need to be removed, as they will be hidden. However, old floor tiles or wall tiles in visible areas may need removing if new tiles are being fitted. The survey will assess this for your specific kitchen.

faqs.q: How much does tiling cost as an addition to kitchen installation?
faqs.a: Tiling cost depends on the area, tile type, and complexity of the cut. Your Install My Kitchen survey will include a separate tiling price if you wish to include this service alongside the kitchen fitting.

title: How Long Does an IKEA Kitchen Take to Install?
metaDescription: IKEA kitchens take 1-2 days longer than pre-assembled kitchens due to flat-pack assembly. A medium IKEA kitchen takes 5-7 days. From Install My Kitchen, Coventry IKEA installation specialists.
answer: An IKEA kitchen typically takes 1–2 days longer than a pre-assembled kitchen of equivalent size. A medium IKEA kitchen (15–20 units) takes 5–7 days — standard installation plus 1–2 days for flat-pack assembly. Every IKEA carcass must be assembled on site before fitting.
bodyHtml:
Why IKEA Takes Longer

IKEA kitchen units arrive in flat-pack form — every carcass, every drawer box, and every internal component must be assembled on site before installation can begin. A pre-assembled kitchen such as Howdens arrives with fully rigid carcasses ready to position and fix. This flat-pack assembly adds a significant amount of time to the overall programme. For a medium kitchen of 15–20 units, the assembly stage alone can take 1–2 full days before any fitting begins. This is not a criticism of IKEA kitchens — the METOD range is well-designed and produces a durable result — but the time implication must be factored into the project plan.

The On-Site Assembly Process

Each IKEA METOD carcass is assembled by fitting the sides, back, and base together using the cam-lock system, then attaching the relevant interior fittings — shelves, drawer runners, and soft-close hinges. Every unit is checked for square before installation begins. Drawer boxes are assembled separately and set aside. Once all carcasses are assembled, the installation process proceeds in the same way as any other kitchen: units are levelled and fixed, worktops are fitted, doors and drawers are hung, and all trims are applied. The IKEA system is generally consistent and logical, which makes assembly efficient once you understand the method.

Preparing Your IKEA Delivery

IKEA kitchen deliveries are often split across multiple orders or arrive in large volumes of flat-pack boxes. Before your fitter arrives, it helps to have the delivery sorted by unit type and placed in or near the kitchen area. Check every box against your order confirmation and report any missing items to IKEA immediately — missing components cause delays that are difficult to recover from during installation. Install My Kitchen installs IKEA kitchens across Coventry and Warwickshire — call 07399 651836 for a fixed quote.

faqs.q: Is IKEA METOD a good quality kitchen?
faqs.a: IKEA METOD is a well-designed kitchen system that offers excellent value. The carcasses are structurally sound, the drawer systems are reliable, and the range of door styles is extensive. Properly installed, an IKEA kitchen performs well and lasts many years.

faqs.q: Can a professional fitter assemble IKEA flat-packs?
faqs.a: Yes — experienced kitchen fitters regularly assemble IKEA flat-packs as part of the installation. The assembly process is factored into the installation quote and programme. You do not need to pre-assemble anything before the fitter arrives.

faqs.q: Does IKEA provide installation?
faqs.a: IKEA offers an installation service through third-party providers, but you are free to use any qualified independent fitter. There is no requirement to use IKEA's own service, and using an independent fitter does not affect your product warranty.

faqs.q: What if IKEA delivers damaged or missing parts?
faqs.a: Contact IKEA immediately if any components are damaged or missing. IKEA is generally good at replacing individual parts, but lead times vary. Report issues as early as possible — ideally before the fitter is due to start — to avoid delays.

title: How to Prepare Your Room for Kitchen Installation
metaDescription: Before installation: complete first-fix services, clear the room, protect floors, confirm delivery is undamaged and ensure parking is available. A complete preparation guide from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Before installation: complete all first-fix plumbing and electrical work, clear the room entirely, protect adjacent floors, confirm delivery is complete and undamaged, and ensure parking is available. A well-prepared room prevents delays and protects your home during installation.
bodyHtml:
First-Fix Services Must Be Done First

First-fix plumbing and electrical work must be completed before kitchen installation begins. First-fix plumbing means the water supply and waste pipes are run to the correct positions but not yet connected to appliances or the sink. First-fix electrics means all circuit cables are run to the correct locations — cooker circuit, socket positions, lighting — but sockets and switches are not yet fitted. If first-fix work is not complete when the fitter arrives, the installation cannot proceed correctly and delays are inevitable. Confirm with your plumber and electrician that first-fix is complete at least 48 hours before the installation start date.

Clearing and Protecting the Room

The kitchen must be completely cleared before the fitter arrives. Remove everything from cupboards, clear worktops entirely, and relocate any freestanding appliances. In a replacement kitchen, the old kitchen will need to be stripped out — this is typically included in the installation service. Protect adjacent floor areas with dust sheets or hardboard, particularly in hallways and doorways where materials will be carried through. Protect any fitted carpets near doorways with hardboard. The installation process generates significant dust from cutting and drilling, so covering nearby soft furnishings and closing internal doors is also advisable.

Day-Before Checklist

The day before installation begins: confirm your full kitchen delivery has arrived and check every item against the delivery note, reporting any damage or missing items to the retailer immediately. Ensure adequate parking is available close to the property — your fitter will arrive with a van full of tools and equipment. Check that the property has a mains electricity supply available and that there is a clear route from the van to the kitchen. If you have pets, arrange for them to be kept away from the work area throughout the installation period.

faqs.q: Does the kitchen fitter remove the old kitchen?
faqs.a: Strip-out of the old kitchen is typically included in the installation service, but confirm this when getting your quote. Strip-out involves removing all existing units, worktops, and appliances. Disposal of the old kitchen may be included or charged separately.

faqs.q: What is first-fix plumbing?
faqs.a: First-fix plumbing is the stage where supply pipes and waste pipes are run to the correct positions within the kitchen but are not yet connected to appliances or a sink. It must be completed before kitchen units are installed.

faqs.q: Do I need to be at home during kitchen installation?
faqs.a: You do not need to be present throughout the day, but you should be available at the start of the first day to walk through the plan with the fitter and confirm any decisions. Being contactable by phone throughout the installation is advisable.

faqs.q: How do I check my kitchen delivery before installation?
faqs.a: Check every box against your order confirmation list when the delivery arrives. Open and inspect any items that appear damaged. Report shortages or damage to the retailer immediately — do not wait until installation day, as replacements can take several days to arrive.

title: What Are Filler Panels in a Kitchen?
metaDescription: Filler panels fill gaps between kitchen units and walls, giving a built-in appearance. Scribing filler panels to irregular walls is a key skill of an experienced kitchen fitter.
answer: Filler panels are thin strips of matching kitchen material used to fill gaps between units and walls, ceilings, or appliances. They give the kitchen a built-in appearance by hiding uneven surfaces. Scribing filler panels to irregular walls is a key skill of any experienced kitchen fitter.
bodyHtml:
Where Filler Panels Are Used

Filler panels appear in several locations throughout a kitchen installation. Tall filler panels are fitted between a tall unit and a wall where the unit cannot be pushed flat against the wall due to obstructions such as pipes or sockets behind. Narrow filler strips are used between a run of units and a chimney breast, corner wall, or return wall where no standard-width unit fits. Appliance fillers are fitted either side of integrated appliances to fill the gap between the appliance housing and adjacent units. In each case, the filler panel must match the kitchen door style and finish exactly, creating the impression that the kitchen was custom-built to fill the space.

The Scribing Technique

When a filler panel meets an uneven or out-of-plumb wall, it must be scribed to fit precisely. Scribing involves holding the panel in its final position and running a compass or scribing tool along the wall surface to transfer its profile onto the panel. The panel is then cut along this line, creating an edge that follows the exact contour of the wall. When the panel is repositioned, it fits tightly against the wall at every point with no visible gaps. This is one of the most visible tests of a kitchen fitter's skill — a poorly scribed panel with visible gaps or an irregular fit immediately detracts from the finished appearance.

Why Filler Panels Matter for the Finished Result

A kitchen without well-fitted filler panels looks unfinished and amateurish. Standard unit widths rarely fill a kitchen space exactly, and the gaps that remain must be filled with matching material. The difference between a kitchen that looks truly built-in and one that looks like a collection of boxes in a room often comes down to how filler panels are handled. At Install My Kitchen, every filler panel is scribed to the wall and fitted with the same precision applied to every other element of the installation.

faqs.q: Are filler panels included in the kitchen supply?
faqs.a: Filler panels are usually ordered separately from the main kitchen units. They are typically supplied in full-length boards that the fitter cuts to size on site. Check with your retailer when placing your order.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a filler panel and a scribe moulding?
faqs.a: A filler panel is a flat piece of matching kitchen material that fills a gap. A scribe moulding is a narrow moulding applied over the edge of a panel or unit to cover small gaps at a wall junction. Both serve a similar purpose but are used in different situations.

faqs.q: Can filler panels be painted?
faqs.a: Painted kitchen filler panels can be painted to match the unit colour. Matt or satin kitchen paint gives the best result. Vinyl-wrapped or high-gloss panels cannot be painted successfully and must be replaced if the finish is changed.

faqs.q: How wide can a filler panel be?
faqs.a: Filler panels are typically available in widths from 50mm to 600mm and can be cut down further on site. For very large gaps, a standard unit of a smaller size is preferable to a very wide filler panel, which can look disproportionate.

title: What Are Kitchen Cornices and Pelmets?
metaDescription: Kitchen cornices are decorative mouldings along the top of wall cabinets. Pelmets fit at the bottom, often concealing lighting. Both are included in Install My Kitchen's standard service.
answer: A cornice is a decorative moulding along the top of wall cabinets where they meet the ceiling. A pelmet is fitted at the bottom of wall cabinets, often concealing under-cabinet lighting. Both give a more refined, furniture-like appearance and are included in our standard installation.
bodyHtml:
What Cornices and Pelmets Do

Cornices and pelmets are the finishing elements that transform a run of wall cabinets from functional storage into something that resembles fine furniture. A cornice is a decorative moulding applied along the top front edge of wall cabinets, bridging the gap between the cabinet top and the ceiling. It gives the kitchen a complete, built-in appearance and hides the uneven junction between the top of the units and the ceiling. A pelmet is fitted along the bottom front edge of wall cabinets, covering the underside of the unit. In practical terms, the pelmet also conceals under-cabinet lighting, creating a clean finish with light visible but the fitting hidden.

Materials and Styles

Cornices and pelmets are supplied by the kitchen retailer in a matching finish to the doors and cabinets. They are available in a range of profiles — from simple flat boards to elaborate traditional mouldings with detailed cross-sections. Modern kitchens typically use a minimal, square-edged cornice that gives a clean contemporary line. Traditional and in-frame kitchens use a more complex ogee or cove profile that reflects the classic furniture aesthetic. The mouldings are mitred at corners to create seamless right-angle joints and scribed at walls to follow any irregularities in the ceiling or wall line.

Lighting Integration with Pelmets

Under-cabinet lighting — whether LED strip, puck lights, or linear fittings — is one of the most functional and aesthetically pleasing additions to a kitchen. The pelmet conceals the fitting while allowing the light to spill onto the worktop below. During installation, the lighting is positioned behind the pelmet before it is fixed, with cables routed back through or behind the wall unit to connect to the switch or driver. If you are planning under-cabinet lighting, this must be agreed before installation begins so that first-fix electrical work is in the correct position.

faqs.q: Do all kitchens need cornices and pelmets?
faqs.a: No — cornices and pelmets are optional finishing elements. Modern handle-less kitchens sometimes omit them entirely for a minimal appearance. However, they greatly improve the finished look of traditional and shaker kitchens and are included in our standard installation where supplied.

faqs.q: Can I add cornices and pelmets after the kitchen is installed?
faqs.a: Yes — cornices and pelmets can be retrofitted after the main installation is complete. However, it is easier and neater to fit them during the initial installation. Retrofitting may require minor adjustments to previously fitted units.

faqs.q: How are cornices mitred at corners?
faqs.a: Cornice corners are cut at 45 degrees on a mitre saw to create a tight, seamless joint. The two pieces meet at a perfect right angle. Any small gap at the joint is filled with matching filler and touched up with paint or finish as required.

faqs.q: What is a light pelmet?
faqs.a: A light pelmet is a pelmet specifically designed to conceal an under-cabinet lighting fitting. It has an open back or channel that allows the light to be positioned behind it and the cable to be routed away cleanly. It looks identical to a standard pelmet from the front.

title: How Much Does Wren Kitchen Installation Cost?
metaDescription: Wren kitchen installation by an independent fitter costs £1,800-£5,500 depending on size. Medium kitchen: £2,800-£4,200. Get a fixed quote from Install My Kitchen across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Wren kitchen installation by an independent fitter typically costs £1,800–£5,500 depending on size. A medium Wren kitchen (13–20 units) costs £2,800–£4,200. Wren's own service tends to be priced at a premium compared to independent fitters.
bodyHtml:
Cost by Kitchen Size

The cost of installing a Wren kitchen by an independent fitter depends primarily on the number of units, the complexity of the layout, and any additional services required such as appliance installation or tiling. A small Wren kitchen of 8–12 units typically costs £1,800–£2,800 to install. A medium kitchen of 13–20 units costs £2,800–£4,200. A large kitchen of 21 or more units, or a kitchen with an island, typically costs £4,200–£5,500 or more. These figures are for installation labour only and do not include worktops, plumbing, or electrical work.

Independent vs Wren's Own Service Cost

Wren offers its own installation service, typically subcontracted through a network of fitters. This service is priced at a premium and is often bundled with the kitchen sale in a way that makes direct cost comparison difficult. Many homeowners who obtain a separate quote from an independent fitter find that installation costs less than Wren's bundled service. The quality of independent installation can also be higher — you can verify the fitter's record, read reviews, and meet them before committing, which is not always possible with a subcontracted service.

Getting a Fixed Wren Installation Quote

Install My Kitchen provides fixed-price installation quotes for all Wren kitchen ranges across Coventry and Warwickshire. Pindi Sahota carries out a pre-installation survey (£195, credited back on booking), reviews your Wren order, and provides a written fixed quote within 24 hours. There are no hidden extras — the price quoted is the price paid. Call 07399 651836 or book your survey online to get started.

faqs.q: Is Wren kitchen installation included in the kitchen price?
faqs.a: No — Wren prices units and installation separately. Installation is an optional add-on. Many customers purchase Wren kitchens and use an independent fitter, which is entirely permitted and does not affect the unit warranty.

faqs.q: How long does Wren kitchen installation take?
faqs.a: A medium Wren kitchen takes 4–6 days to install. Wren supplies rigid pre-assembled carcasses, which install efficiently. Large kitchens or those with complex layouts will take longer.

faqs.q: Does Wren use their own fitters or subcontractors?
faqs.a: Wren's installation service uses subcontracted fitters rather than directly employed staff. The quality of the work can therefore vary. Using an independently chosen fitter gives you greater control over who installs your kitchen.

faqs.q: What is not included in a kitchen installation quote?
faqs.a: A standard installation quote covers cabinet assembly, unit fitting, door and drawer fitting, plinths, cornices, and appliance positioning. It does not include plumbing connections, electrical connections, gas work, or tiling — these are quoted separately.

title: How Much Does a Howdens Kitchen Cost to Fit?
metaDescription: Howdens kitchen installation costs £1,800-£5,000 for labour. Medium kitchen: £2,800-£4,000. Rigid pre-assembled units keep installation efficient. Fixed quotes from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Howdens kitchen installation typically costs £1,800–£5,000 for labour depending on size. A medium Howdens kitchen (13–20 units) typically costs £2,800–£4,000. Howdens rigid pre-assembled units install efficiently, keeping labour time and cost competitive.
bodyHtml:
Howdens Installation Cost by Size

Howdens kitchens use rigid pre-assembled carcasses, which install faster than flat-pack alternatives and keep labour costs competitive. A small Howdens kitchen of 8–12 units typically costs £1,800–£2,800 to install. A medium kitchen of 13–20 units costs £2,800–£4,000. A large kitchen or one with an island unit costs £4,000–£5,000 or more. These figures cover installation labour only. Worktop supply, plumbing, electrical, and gas connections are costed separately depending on the scope of work involved.

Why Howdens Installs Efficiently

Howdens carcasses arrive pre-assembled and rigid, meaning the fitter can begin positioning and levelling immediately without a flat-pack assembly phase. The Howdens range uses a consistent fixing system across all carcass types, which an experienced fitter can work through quickly and confidently. The quality of Howdens units — robust carcasses, reliable drawer runners, good-quality hinges — also means that components work correctly first time, reducing the time spent on adjustments. Over 30 years, Pindi Sahota has installed hundreds of Howdens kitchens and works efficiently with their product range.

Getting a Fixed Howdens Quote

Install My Kitchen provides fixed-price quotes for Howdens kitchen installation across Coventry and Warwickshire. Bring your Howdens order summary to your survey appointment or provide it in advance, and we will price the installation accurately against the actual units being supplied. Call 07399 651836 to book your survey (£195, credited back on booking). You will receive a written fixed quote within 24 hours of the survey visit.

faqs.q: Can a homeowner buy directly from Howdens?
faqs.a: Howdens is a trade-only supplier. Homeowners cannot purchase directly — the account must be held by a builder, fitter, or other trade professional. Many kitchen fitters, including Install My Kitchen, can purchase Howdens kitchens on behalf of clients.

faqs.q: Is Howdens a good quality kitchen?
faqs.a: Howdens kitchens are widely regarded as excellent quality for the price. The rigid carcasses are robust, the drawer systems are reliable, and the range is extensive. They represent good value across the mid-range market.

faqs.q: Does Howdens offer installation?
faqs.a: Howdens does not offer a consumer installation service. As a trade-only supplier, they sell exclusively to the trade and expect builders or fitters to carry out installation independently.

faqs.q: How do I get a Howdens kitchen without a trade account?
faqs.a: Ask your kitchen fitter to supply the Howdens kitchen on your behalf. The fitter purchases through their trade account and passes the cost on to you. Install My Kitchen can advise on this approach — call 07399 651836 to discuss.

title: How Much Does It Cost to Install an IKEA Kitchen?
metaDescription: IKEA kitchen installation costs £2,200-£6,000 for labour in the UK. Flat-pack assembly adds 15-25% vs pre-assembled kitchens. Medium IKEA kitchen: £3,000-£4,800 to install.
answer: IKEA kitchen installation typically costs £2,200–£6,000 for labour in the UK. Flat-pack assembly adds approximately 15–25% to costs compared to pre-assembled kitchens. A medium IKEA kitchen installation (15–20 units) typically costs £3,000–£4,800.
bodyHtml:
IKEA Installation Cost by Size

IKEA kitchen installation is priced higher than pre-assembled equivalents due to the flat-pack assembly time required. A small IKEA kitchen of 8–12 units typically costs £2,200–£3,200 to install. A medium kitchen of 15–20 units costs £3,000–£4,800. A large kitchen of 21 or more units or one with an island typically costs £4,800–£6,000 or more. The flat-pack assembly element adds approximately 15–25% to the cost compared to an equivalent rigid-carcass kitchen. These are labour-only figures — worktops, plumbing, and electrical work are costed separately.

Why IKEA Costs More to Install

Every IKEA carcass must be assembled by the fitter on site before installation can begin. A typical 15-unit IKEA kitchen may involve assembling 15 carcasses, 15 sets of drawer boxes, and dozens of internal fittings before a single unit is positioned on the wall. This assembly phase adds 1–2 days to the programme compared to a pre-assembled kitchen of the same size. The additional time is reflected in the installation cost. Despite the higher installation cost, IKEA kitchens often represent good overall value because the unit cost is lower than many comparable pre-assembled ranges.

Getting a Fixed IKEA Installation Price

Install My Kitchen provides fixed-price quotes for IKEA kitchen installation across Coventry and Warwickshire. Provide your IKEA kitchen plan or order reference and we will assess the scope of work and provide a written price. Call 07399 651836 or book your survey online. As with all our quotes, the price is fixed — no additions on completion.

faqs.q: Is IKEA METOD easy to assemble?
faqs.a: IKEA METOD carcasses follow a consistent assembly method that becomes efficient once understood. An experienced fitter assembles them quickly and accurately. The system is well-engineered, and the cam-lock fixings provide a reliable, square result when assembled correctly.

faqs.q: Does IKEA charge more for installation than independent fitters?
faqs.a: IKEA's own installation service, provided through third parties, is often competitively priced but with limited fitter selection. An independent fitter may be comparable in price with the advantage that you can assess their experience and read reviews before committing.

faqs.q: What if IKEA parts are missing during installation?
faqs.a: Missing IKEA parts can cause significant delays. IKEA will replace missing items but lead times vary. Check your delivery against the full order before the fitter arrives and contact IKEA immediately about any shortages.

faqs.q: Can I pre-assemble IKEA carcasses before the fitter arrives?
faqs.a: You can, but it is not recommended unless you are confident in the assembly process. Incorrectly assembled carcasses can be difficult to dismantle and may be damaged in the process. It is usually more efficient to let the fitter assemble as part of the installation.

title: What's the Difference Between a Kitchen Fitter and Installer?
metaDescription: Kitchen fitter and kitchen installer are interchangeable terms in the UK — both refer to the same skilled tradesperson. There is no meaningful distinction between the two titles.
answer: The terms kitchen fitter and kitchen installer are used interchangeably in the UK. Both refer to a skilled tradesperson who assembles and installs kitchen cabinetry, worktops, and appliances. There is no meaningful distinction between the two titles.
bodyHtml:
Fitter vs Installer — The Same Trade

In the UK kitchen industry, the words "fitter" and "installer" describe the same role and the same set of skills. There is no recognised distinction between the two terms in trade usage, training frameworks, or industry bodies. Both titles describe a tradesperson who takes kitchen units from delivery to completion — assembling, positioning, levelling, and finishing a kitchen to a professional standard. Some companies use one term over the other as a matter of preference, but a client searching for a kitchen fitter and a client searching for a kitchen installer are looking for exactly the same person.

What the Title Tells You

Neither title in itself tells you anything about experience, quality, or the scope of work the tradesperson is capable of. A kitchen fitter with 30 years of experience and a new installer with six months of experience would both describe themselves using either term. What matters is not the title but the person behind it — their experience, their portfolio of completed work, the reviews left by previous clients, and the quality of the fixed written quote they provide. Always ask to see examples of previous installations and check online reviews before committing to any kitchen fitter or installer.

What Actually Matters When Choosing

When selecting a kitchen fitter or installer, focus on three things: experience with the specific type of kitchen you are having installed, a written fixed-price quote that covers the full scope of work, and a workmanship warranty. Install My Kitchen's Pindi Sahota has 30 years of kitchen installation experience across all major brands and kitchen types. To get a fixed quote, call 07399 651836 or book a survey online. We serve all Coventry postcodes and Warwickshire.

faqs.q: Is a kitchen installer the same as a kitchen fitter?
faqs.a: Yes — the two terms are entirely interchangeable in the UK. Both describe a tradesperson who professionally assembles and installs kitchen units, worktops, and appliances. There is no meaningful distinction between them.

faqs.q: Do kitchen fitters need a qualification?
faqs.a: There is no mandatory licensing requirement to work as a kitchen fitter in the UK. However, many professional fitters hold NVQ qualifications in fitted interiors or carpentry. Experience and a verifiable portfolio of completed work are the most reliable indicators of quality.

faqs.q: Does a kitchen fitter also do plumbing and electrics?
faqs.a: Some kitchen fitters offer basic plumbing and electrical work as part of their service. However, gas connections must be carried out by a Gas Safe registered engineer, and full rewiring must be done by a qualified electrician. Always clarify the scope of what is included.

faqs.q: How do I find a reliable kitchen fitter near me?
faqs.a: Search online for local kitchen fitters and read reviews on Google or Checkatrade. Ask for references from previous clients, and always get a written fixed-price quote. Install My Kitchen covers Coventry and Warwickshire — call 07399 651836 to book a survey (£195, credited back on booking).

title: How Do You Level Kitchen Units on an Uneven Floor?
metaDescription: Kitchen units are levelled using adjustable feet, laser levels and packing pieces. The first unit sets the reference for the entire kitchen. Expert advice from Install My Kitchen, Coventry.
answer: Kitchen units are levelled on uneven floors using adjustable feet that screw in or out, combined with a spirit or laser level. Packing pieces compensate where adjustment is insufficient. The first unit must be perfectly level — it sets the reference for the entire kitchen.
bodyHtml:
Adjustable Feet and Laser Levels

Every modern kitchen base unit is fitted with adjustable plastic feet — typically four per unit — that can be screwed up or down to raise or lower each corner independently. Using a laser level set to project a horizontal reference line at worktop height, the fitter adjusts each unit until the top of the carcass is exactly on the reference line. This process must be meticulous, because once units are fixed and the worktop is applied, any deviation becomes permanent and visible. The first unit installed is the most important — it sets the reference from which every subsequent unit is levelled. A mistake at this stage propagates across the entire run.

Packing Pieces for Extreme Variation

Adjustable feet typically provide 100–150mm of height adjustment. In most UK homes this is sufficient, but older properties can have floors that deviate by more than this within a run of units. Where the floor falls beyond the adjustment range, packing pieces — typically hardwood or plywood — are placed beneath the adjustable feet to extend the effective range. This is common in Victorian and Edwardian terraces, where floors can be significantly lower at the wall than in the centre of the room. Packing pieces must be stable and non-compressible — soft materials will compress over time and cause the kitchen to move.

What Our Survey Finds About Coventry Floors

Coventry's housing stock includes a wide range of construction ages and types. Victorian terraces in Radford and Stoke, inter-war semis in Coundon and Earlsdon, and postwar estates across the city all present different floor conditions. Pindi Sahota's survey visit assesses the floor level across the full kitchen area and identifies any significant variation before installation begins. This means there are no surprises on installation day — the right approach and materials are prepared in advance.

faqs.q: What if my floor slopes more than the adjustable feet can compensate for?
faqs.a: Packing pieces are used beneath the feet to extend the effective adjustment range. In extreme cases, a screed or self-levelling compound may be applied to the floor before installation, though this is rarely required in domestic kitchens.

faqs.q: Should kitchen floors be levelled before installation?
faqs.a: Minor floor variations are handled by the adjustable feet and do not require any floor preparation. Significant variations of more than 20–25mm over the kitchen area may benefit from self-levelling compound. The survey will identify if this is necessary.

faqs.q: How level do kitchen units need to be?
faqs.a: Kitchen units should be level to within 1–2mm across their full width and from front to back. This tolerance ensures doors close correctly, drawers operate smoothly, and worktops sit flat. A laser level makes achieving this consistently straightforward.

faqs.q: Can I see the adjustable feet once the kitchen is installed?
faqs.a: No — the plinths fitted along the base of the units conceal the adjustable feet completely. The plinth clips onto the feet and can be removed for access to plumbing beneath the units if required.

title: Is Kitchen Installation Expensive? An Honest Answer
metaDescription: Kitchen installation costs £1,800-£7,500 but protects an investment of £5,000-£30,000 in units. Quality installation shows in every detail. An honest assessment from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Kitchen installation costs £1,800–£7,500 — significant, but it protects an investment of £5,000–£30,000 in units. A poor installation shows in every door alignment, every gap, every worktop joint. Quality installation is an investment that pays for itself in the finished result.
bodyHtml:
What You Are Actually Paying For

Kitchen installation labour typically costs £1,800–£7,500 depending on size, complexity, and the scope of additional services. To put this in context: a typical kitchen unit purchase costs £5,000–£30,000. The installation transforms those units into a functional, beautiful kitchen — or not. A professional installation includes precise levelling and alignment, accurate scribing to walls, correct door and drawer calibration, tight worktop joints, neat plinth and cornice fitting, and a full snagging check. These are skilled tasks that require experience, the right tools, and attention to detail that genuinely matters in the finished result.

Why Cheap Installation Costs More Long-Term

The consequences of a poor installation are visible every day and difficult to correct without dismantling and refitting. Misaligned doors, visible gaps at walls, poorly jointed worktops, loose plinths, and hinges that do not close correctly are not cosmetic problems that can be painted over — they require the fitter to return and redo the work correctly. In the worst cases, a poorly installed kitchen may need to be partially or fully dismantled and reinstalled. The cost of remedial work, combined with the disruption, typically exceeds any saving made by choosing a cheap installer in the first place.

How to Get Quality Without Overpaying

A fixed written quote from a recommended independent fitter is usually better value than a bundled retailer installation service. Compare two or three fixed quotes from fitters with verifiable local experience and genuine reviews. Install My Kitchen provides fixed-price surveys and quotes for Coventry and Warwickshire — survey is £195, credited back on booking. Call 07399 651836 to book.

faqs.q: Is kitchen installation VAT-rated?
faqs.a: Kitchen installation by a sole trader or small business that is not VAT-registered is not subject to VAT. VAT-registered businesses must charge VAT at 20%. When comparing quotes, check whether VAT is included or excluded.

faqs.q: What is the most expensive part of kitchen installation?
faqs.a: Labour time is the largest component of installation cost. Complex worktop cutting, scribing to difficult walls, and in-frame kitchen fitting are the most time-intensive tasks. Worktop templating and stone fitting are often the most expensive individual elements.

faqs.q: Can I save money by doing some kitchen work myself?
faqs.a: Some homeowners choose to paint walls, handle deliveries, or remove the old kitchen themselves to reduce overall cost. These tasks are straightforward. Attempting to install the units yourself without experience is not recommended — errors are costly to correct.

faqs.q: Does kitchen installation add value to a home?
faqs.a: A well-installed modern kitchen is widely recognised as one of the most effective home improvements for adding value. Estate agents consistently report that a quality kitchen is a significant factor in buyer decisions and offers received.

title: Can I Use Any Fitter for My Wren Kitchen?
metaDescription: Yes — Wren does not require you to use their own installation service. Any independent fitter can install your Wren kitchen without affecting the unit warranty.
answer: Yes — Wren does not require you to use their own installation service. Any qualified independent fitter can install your Wren kitchen. The unit warranty covers manufacturing defects and is not affected by who carries out the installation.
bodyHtml:
Wren's Warranty Policy Confirmed

Wren Kitchens offers an installation service as an optional add-on, but this service is not compulsory and there is no requirement to use it. The unit warranty provided by Wren covers manufacturing defects in the cabinets, doors, and hardware. This warranty applies regardless of who installs the kitchen. Installation errors — such as a damaged hinge caused during fitting — would be the installer's responsibility rather than Wren's, but this is true whether the installer is Wren's own service or an independent fitter. Many Wren customers choose independent installation for cost, flexibility, or because they already have a trusted fitter.

Benefits of an Independent Wren Fitter

Using an independent fitter for your Wren kitchen gives you several advantages. You can choose a fitter based on verified local reviews and personal recommendations, visit completed installations if needed, and obtain a fixed written quote that covers the exact scope of your project. Wren's own installation service is subcontracted to a network of fitters whose quality can vary. An independently chosen fitter is personally accountable to you throughout the project and afterward — there is no call centre between you and the person responsible for your kitchen.

Installing Wren in Coventry and Warwickshire

Install My Kitchen provides independent installation of all Wren kitchen ranges across Coventry and Warwickshire. Pindi Sahota has installed many Wren kitchens and is familiar with the full product range. Wren rigid carcasses install efficiently, and the quality of the units is consistently good. To get a fixed quote for your Wren kitchen installation, call 07399 651836 or book a survey through our website.

faqs.q: Does using an independent fitter void my Wren warranty?
faqs.a: No — Wren's unit warranty covers manufacturing defects regardless of who installs the kitchen. The warranty is a product warranty, not conditional on using Wren's installation service.

faqs.q: Is Wren installation more expensive than using an independent fitter?
faqs.a: Wren's installation is often priced at a premium compared to an equivalent independent fitter. Getting a separate installation quote allows you to make a direct cost comparison before committing.

faqs.q: How does a Wren kitchen fitter access the kitchen details?
faqs.a: Wren provides a detailed order summary and kitchen plan with every purchase. Share these documents with your independent fitter at survey stage so they can assess the full scope of work and price accurately.

faqs.q: Are Wren kitchens supplied pre-assembled?
faqs.a: Wren kitchens are supplied as rigid pre-assembled carcasses rather than flat-pack, which makes installation efficient. Door fronts, drawer fronts, and hardware are supplied separately and fitted on site.

title: Can I Use an Independent Fitter for My Howdens Kitchen?
metaDescription: Yes — Howdens has no consumer installation service so independent fitters are the standard route. Install My Kitchen provides independent Howdens installation across Coventry and Warwickshire.
answer: Yes — Howdens has no consumer installation service, so using an independent fitter is the standard route for homeowners. Most Howdens kitchens are installed by independent fitters or builders. Install My Kitchen provides independent Howdens installation across Coventry and Warwickshire.
bodyHtml:
Howdens Trade-Only Confirmed

Howdens Joinery operates exclusively as a trade supplier. There is no consumer-facing installation service — Howdens sells to builders, joiners, and kitchen fitters through trade accounts, and the expectation is that the trade professional who purchases the kitchen will either install it themselves or engage another tradesperson to do so. This model means that for homeowners, an independent fitter is not just permitted but is essentially the only route. There is no alternative Howdens installation service to compare against — using an independent fitter such as Install My Kitchen is the standard approach for the vast majority of Howdens kitchens installed in the UK.

Finding an Independent Howdens Fitter

Because Howdens kitchens must be purchased through a trade account, the simplest route for homeowners is to engage a kitchen fitter who holds a Howdens trade account and can purchase on their behalf. This allows you to visit your local Howdens depot with your fitter, work through the design, and have the kitchen purchased and delivered directly. Alternatively, if you already have a Howdens account through a builder or other trade contact, purchase the kitchen yourself and engage an independent fitter for installation only.

Getting a Quote for Howdens Installation

Install My Kitchen provides independent Howdens installation across Coventry and Warwickshire. Pindi Sahota is experienced with all Howdens ranges and can advise on the purchase process if required. Fixed installation quotes are provided within 24 hours of your survey. Call 07399 651836 or book online.

faqs.q: Can I visit Howdens without a trade account?
faqs.a: Howdens depots are open to trade account holders only. Homeowners must visit with or through a trade professional who holds an account. Your kitchen fitter can accompany you or purchase on your behalf.

faqs.q: How does Howdens pricing work for homeowners?
faqs.a: Howdens pricing is set at trade level and includes volume discounts. Your fitter will provide a quotation based on the Howdens product cost plus their installation labour. The total is typically competitive with comparable pre-assembled ranges from other retailers.

faqs.q: Is Howdens better quality than Wren?
faqs.a: Both Howdens and Wren are quality mid-market kitchen ranges. Howdens is trade-only and has a strong reputation among professional fitters for robust carcasses and reliable components. Wren is consumer-facing with a wider showroom network. Both are good choices.

faqs.q: Can Install My Kitchen supply a Howdens kitchen?
faqs.a: Yes — Install My Kitchen can purchase Howdens kitchens through our trade account on your behalf. This simplifies the process considerably. Call 07399 651836 to discuss supply and fit options for Howdens kitchens.

title: What Exactly Does Kitchen Installation Include?
metaDescription: Kitchen installation includes cabinet assembly, worktops, doors, drawers, handles, plinths, cornices, appliances and snagging. Plumbing, electrical and gas are separate. Full guide from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Professional kitchen installation includes cabinet assembly and installation, worktop fitting, door and drawer fitting, handle fitting, plinths, cornices and all trims, appliance and sink positioning, and a full snagging check. It does not include plumbing, electrical, or gas connections.
bodyHtml:
Full List of What Is Included

A professional kitchen installation covers the following scope of work: delivery check and sorting of units on site; assembly of flat-pack carcasses where applicable; levelling and fixing of all base units; fitting and fixing of all wall units; cutting and fitting of all worktops including jointing and cut-outs for sink and hob; hanging and calibrating all doors and drawer fronts; fitting all handles and knobs; fitting plinths, cornices, pelmets, and all trim items; scribing filler panels to walls; positioning integrated appliances within their housings; positioning sink within the worktop cut-out; and a full snagging check at completion to identify and correct any remaining issues.

What Is Not Included — and Why

Kitchen installation labour does not include plumbing connections, electrical connections, or gas connections. These elements require specific qualifications — Gas Safe registration for gas work, Part P notification for certain electrical work, and professional liability insurance for plumbing. Specialist trades carry these qualifications and the associated liability. Attempting to have an unqualified person carry out these connections is dangerous and potentially illegal. Your project will require a plumber to connect the sink and any water-fed appliances, and an electrician for hardwired appliances. These tradespeople are co-ordinated around the kitchen installation programme.

Confirming Scope in Your Written Quote

Always confirm exactly what is included and excluded in your written kitchen installation quote before signing. A good quote lists every item of work covered and clearly states what falls outside the scope. Install My Kitchen provides detailed written quotes that leave no room for ambiguity. Call 07399 651836 to book your survey across Coventry and Warwickshire.

faqs.q: Does kitchen installation include removing the old kitchen?
faqs.a: Strip-out of an existing kitchen is included in Install My Kitchen's standard service. This covers the removal of all existing units, worktops, and appliances. Disposal of the old kitchen is confirmed at survey stage.

faqs.q: Is appliance installation included in the kitchen fitting quote?
faqs.a: Appliance positioning and fitting into integrated housings is included. Gas and electrical connections to appliances are not included and must be carried out by appropriately qualified tradespeople.

faqs.q: Does the kitchen fitter connect the sink?
faqs.a: The kitchen fitter positions the sink in the worktop cut-out and may connect overflow fittings. The water supply and waste connections are typically made by a plumber after the worktop and sink are in position.

faqs.q: What happens during the snagging check?
faqs.a: Snagging is a final inspection of the completed kitchen. The fitter checks every door alignment, every drawer operation, every joint and seal, and every trim fitting. Any issues identified are corrected before handover.

title: How Many Days Does It Take to Fit a Kitchen?
metaDescription: Small kitchen: 3-4 days. Medium: 4-6 days. Large: 6-10 days. IKEA adds 1-2 days. Stone worktops need a return visit 5-10 days after templating. Timescales from Install My Kitchen.
answer: Kitchen fitting takes 3–4 days for small (8–12 units), 4–6 days for medium (13–20 units), and 6–10 days for large (21+ units) kitchens. IKEA adds 1–2 days. Stone worktops require a return visit 5–10 days after templating.
bodyHtml:
Days by Kitchen Size

Installation time is driven primarily by the number of units and the complexity of the layout. A small kitchen of 8–12 units in a simple L-shape or galley layout takes 3–4 days. A medium kitchen of 13–20 units with a more complex arrangement takes 4–6 days. A large kitchen of 21 or more units, or any kitchen incorporating an island, typically takes 6–10 days. These figures assume a pre-assembled kitchen with laminate or timber worktops. IKEA flat-pack kitchens add 1–2 days due to on-site carcass assembly. In-frame kitchens add approximately 20–30% to the standard programme.

What Extends the Programme

Several factors can extend the installation programme beyond the standard timescales. Stone worktops require templating after base units are installed, followed by a 5–10 working day fabrication period before the worktop is returned for fitting. This creates a gap in the middle of the programme during which the kitchen is not fully functional. Significant floor or wall irregularities that require additional preparation time will add to the programme. Appliance deliveries that do not arrive on time, or missing unit components, can also cause delays. These factors are identified at survey stage and factored into the programme planning.

Building Your Full Project Schedule

A complete kitchen project — including first-fix services, delivery, installation, stone templating, plumbing connections, and final tiling — typically takes 3–5 weeks from the start of first-fix work to final completion. Planning this sequence carefully, with each trade booked in the correct order, is essential. Install My Kitchen advises clients on programme planning during the survey visit. Call 07399 651836 to book your survey across Coventry and Warwickshire.

faqs.q: How long does a small kitchen take to fit?
faqs.a: A small kitchen of 8–12 units in a straightforward layout typically takes 3–4 days to install. This assumes a pre-assembled kitchen with standard worktops. IKEA or in-frame construction will add to this timescale.

faqs.q: Does the same fitter work every day during the installation?
faqs.a: Yes — at Install My Kitchen, Pindi Sahota is on site throughout the installation. There is no handover between different fitters partway through the project. This ensures consistency and accountability throughout.

faqs.q: Can a kitchen be installed in a single day?
faqs.a: Very small kitchens of 5–8 units — typically galley kitchens or studio flat kitchens — can sometimes be completed in a single day. Most domestic kitchens require multiple days. A single-day installation typically means the kitchen is small and simple in layout.

faqs.q: What happens if the installation takes longer than expected?
faqs.a: Install My Kitchen provides a realistic programme estimate at survey stage. If unforeseen issues arise — such as discovering concealed defects in the existing structure — we discuss the situation openly and agree the best course of action before proceeding.

title: Who Is Responsible If Kitchen Installation Goes Wrong?
metaDescription: Manufacturing defects are the retailer's responsibility. Installation errors are the fitter's responsibility. Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installations.
answer: Responsibility depends on the cause. Manufacturing defects in units are the retailer's responsibility. Installation errors are the fitter's responsibility. Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installations covering installation defects.
bodyHtml:
Retailer Warranty vs Workmanship Warranty

Two separate warranties apply to a newly installed kitchen. The retailer's warranty — typically 5–25 years depending on the brand — covers defects that arise from the manufacturing process. A door that warps without cause, a hinge that fails under normal use, a drawer box that breaks without misuse — these are manufacturing defects and the retailer is responsible for replacing the components. The fitter's workmanship warranty covers defects that arise from the installation itself — incorrect levelling, a poorly jointed worktop, a damaged carcass side caused during fitting. These are installation errors and the fitter is responsible for correcting them.

What Each Warranty Covers

Understanding the boundary between the two warranties matters when something goes wrong. If a door warps, it is likely a manufacturing issue — contact the retailer. If a door will not close correctly because the hinge was fitted at the wrong angle, it is an installation issue — contact the fitter. In practice, the distinction is usually clear. A professional fitter will not attempt to attribute installation errors to manufacturing defects, and a reputable retailer will not attempt to deny a genuine warranty claim. Problems arise most often when either party is unclear about their responsibilities — a written quote from the fitter and a clear written warranty from the retailer prevents ambiguity.

Our 12-Month Workmanship Guarantee

Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installations. If any aspect of the installation proves defective within 12 months — a joint that opens, a unit that has moved, a plinth that has come away — we return to correct it at no charge. This guarantee reflects our confidence in the quality of our work. Call 07399 651836 to discuss this in more detail.

faqs.q: What if I am not sure whether a problem is a manufacturing or installation defect?
faqs.a: Contact your fitter first — they can inspect the problem and advise on the likely cause. If the cause is clearly manufacturing-related, they can help you document the issue to present to the retailer.

faqs.q: Can I claim against both the retailer and the fitter?
faqs.a: You can contact both parties, but responsibility will ultimately rest with one or the other depending on the cause. In most cases, a professional fitter and a reputable retailer will resolve the matter without requiring the customer to pursue a formal dispute.

faqs.q: What does a 12-month workmanship warranty cover?
faqs.a: A 12-month workmanship warranty covers defects that result directly from the installation — levelling issues, fitting errors, joint failures, and trim problems. It does not cover damage caused by the homeowner or normal wear and tear.

faqs.q: What happens if the fitter has gone out of business?
faqs.a: If your fitter has ceased trading, pursuing a warranty claim becomes difficult. This is one reason to choose a well-established local fitter with a proven track record rather than the cheapest option available. Install My Kitchen has operated in Coventry for 30 years.

title: Do I Need to Move Out During Kitchen Installation?
metaDescription: No — you do not need to move out during kitchen installation. Plan for 3-10 days without a functional kitchen and set up temporary facilities. Practical guide from Install My Kitchen.
answer: No — you do not need to move out. You will be without a functional kitchen for 3–10 days, so temporary cooking facilities are advisable. The installation team works during normal hours and leaves the property secure each evening.
bodyHtml:
Living In During Installation

The vast majority of kitchen installations are carried out while the homeowner remains in the property. You do not need to vacate your home. The fitter works during normal daytime hours and will leave the property secure each evening. At the end of each working day, any hazardous materials such as sharp offcuts will be cleared from the floor, tools will be stored safely, and any immediate mess contained. The kitchen area will not be functional during the installation period, but the rest of the house remains unaffected.

Setting Up a Temporary Kitchen

Planning for a temporary kitchen before installation begins makes the disruption much more manageable. A microwave, kettle, and a small table in another room give you the basics for daily meals. A cool box or a second-hand mini fridge keeps essentials fresh. If your property has a utility room with a sink, this can serve as a basic washing-up facility during the installation period. For a short installation of 3–4 days, many households manage perfectly well on sandwiches, supermarket prepared meals, and takeaways without any temporary arrangement.
