For longer programmes of a week or more, a temporary setup is more worthwhile.

Managing Dust and Disruption

Kitchen installation generates dust from cutting, drilling, and grinding. Closing the doors between the kitchen area and the rest of the house significantly reduces the spread of dust. Dust sheets in doorways and hallways protect floors and soft furnishings. The fitter will manage dust as part of the daily work, but some spread is unavoidable. Plan to clean adjacent areas at the end of each day. Children and pets should be kept away from the work area throughout for safety reasons.

faqs.q: How long will I be without a kitchen during installation?
faqs.a: Most domestic kitchens are out of use for 3–10 days during installation. Small kitchens may be done in 3–4 days. Large kitchens or those with stone worktops requiring templating may be out of full use for up to 2–3 weeks total.

faqs.q: Will the fitter make a mess?
faqs.a: Some mess is inevitable during kitchen installation — cutting generates dust and debris. A professional fitter tidies the work area at the end of each day, removes waste, and leaves the property in a reasonable state each evening.

faqs.q: Is it safe to be in the house during kitchen installation?
faqs.a: Yes — it is safe to remain in the house during kitchen installation. Keep children and pets away from the work area. If gas work is being carried out at the same time, your Gas Safe engineer will advise on any ventilation requirements.

faqs.q: Can I use my bathroom during kitchen installation?
faqs.a: Yes — the bathroom and all other rooms in the property remain fully usable during kitchen installation. Only the kitchen itself is unavailable.

title: What Guarantee Does Kitchen Installation Come With?
metaDescription: Unit guarantees of 5-25 years are provided by the retailer. Installation workmanship should be separately guaranteed by the fitter. Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty.
answer: Kitchen unit guarantees (5–25 years) cover manufacturing defects and are provided by the retailer. Installation workmanship should be guaranteed separately by the fitter. Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installations.
bodyHtml:
Retailer Unit Guarantee Explained

All major UK kitchen retailers provide a product guarantee on their units. Howdens offers a 25-year structural guarantee on carcasses. Wren provides a 25-year guarantee on carcasses and a 10-year guarantee on doors. IKEA offers a 25-year guarantee on METOD frames. Magnet and other mid-market retailers typically offer 5–10 year guarantees. These guarantees cover manufacturing defects — failures that arise from the product itself under normal use, not damage caused during installation or by the homeowner. Guarantees are registered with the retailer and follow the product, not the installer.

What a Workmanship Warranty Covers

A workmanship warranty from your fitter covers the quality of the installation itself. It is entirely separate from the retailer's product guarantee and covers different things. A workmanship warranty should cover: levelling and alignment remaining correct, worktop joints remaining tight and sealed, doors and drawers continuing to operate correctly, plinths and cornices remaining fixed, and silicone seals remaining intact. The duration of a workmanship warranty varies between fitters. Some offer 6 months, others 12 months or more. Always ask for this warranty in writing before committing to an installer.

Our 12-Month Guarantee

Install My Kitchen provides a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installations, confirmed in writing. Within the 12-month period, if any element of the installation is found to be defective as a result of our work, we return to correct it at no cost. This guarantee covers all aspects of the installation from unit levelling to worktop jointing to trim fitting. It reflects 30 years of confidence in the quality of our workmanship. To discuss our warranty in detail, call 07399 651836.

faqs.q: Do I need to register my kitchen unit guarantee?
faqs.a: Some retailers require guarantee registration — Wren, for example, has an online registration process. Others register automatically at purchase. Check with your retailer at the time of purchase and keep your receipt and order confirmation.

faqs.q: Does the kitchen guarantee transfer if I sell the house?
faqs.a: Most retailer guarantees are linked to the product at the property rather than to the individual purchaser, meaning they transfer with the property. Check the specific terms with your retailer — this can be a selling point when marketing your home.

faqs.q: What is not covered by a workmanship warranty?
faqs.a: Workmanship warranties cover defects resulting from the installation. They do not cover damage caused by the homeowner, normal wear and tear, or issues arising from subsequent trades working in the same area.

faqs.q: Is a verbal guarantee from a fitter sufficient?
faqs.a: No — always get the workmanship warranty in writing as part of your installation quote or contract. A verbal assurance is difficult to enforce. Install My Kitchen confirms all warranty terms in writing before installation begins.

title: How to Book a Kitchen Fitter in Coventry
metaDescription: Book a kitchen fitter in Coventry by calling Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836 or using our online form. Survey within 1-2 weeks, fixed quote within 24 hours. All CV postcodes covered.
answer: To book a kitchen fitter in Coventry: call Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836 or use our online survey booking form. Survey appointments available within 1–2 weeks across all Coventry postcodes and Warwickshire. You receive a fixed quote within 24 hours of your survey.
bodyHtml:
Step 1 — Book Your Survey

The first step is to book a survey appointment. Call Install My Kitchen on 07399 651836 or use the online booking form on our website. Survey appointments are available within 1–2 weeks across all Coventry postcodes — CV1 through to CV6 and beyond — as well as across Warwickshire including Kenilworth, Leamington Spa, Nuneaton, and Rugby. The survey is carried out by Pindi Sahota personally, with 30 years of kitchen installation experience. During the survey, Pindi assesses the room dimensions, floor and wall conditions, the scope of work, and discusses any specific requirements you have for the project.

Step 2 — Receive Your Fixed Quote

Within 24 hours of your survey visit, you will receive a written fixed-price quote covering the full scope of installation work discussed. The quote is itemised and leaves no room for ambiguity — what is included is clearly stated, and what falls outside the scope is equally clear. There are no hidden extras and the price quoted is the price paid. If your project includes additional elements such as tiling or appliance connections by a separate trade, we can advise on coordinating these within the programme. The quote is valid for 30 days from issue.

Step 3 — Confirm and Installation Day

Once you are happy with the quote, confirm by calling 07399 651836 or by replying to the quote email. We will agree a start date that fits your project programme — typically 2–4 weeks from confirmation, allowing time for your kitchen delivery to arrive and for any first-fix work to be completed. On installation day, Pindi arrives promptly and walks through the plan with you before starting. Install My Kitchen serves all of Coventry and Warwickshire — 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry CV6 2AH.

faqs.q: How quickly can Install My Kitchen start after the survey?
faqs.a: Start dates are typically available within 2–4 weeks of confirming your installation. This allows time for kitchen delivery and any first-fix work to be completed before installation begins. Urgent bookings can sometimes be accommodated — call to discuss availability.

faqs.q: What should I have ready for the survey?
faqs.a: Bring your kitchen order summary or design plan to the survey if you have it. If you have not yet ordered the kitchen, we can still survey and quote based on your anticipated layout. The more detail you can provide, the more accurate the quote.

faqs.q: Does Install My Kitchen cover all Coventry postcodes?
faqs.a: Yes — Install My Kitchen covers all Coventry CV postcodes including CV1, CV2, CV3, CV4, CV5, CV6, and surrounding Warwickshire areas. Call 07399 651836 to confirm coverage for your postcode.

faqs.q: Is there a call-out fee for the survey?
faqs.a: The survey fee is £195, credited in full against your installation balance when you book. Pindi Sahota visits the property, assesses the scope of work, and provides a written fixed quote within 24 hours.
